title: Nova Lingua Effect Trace
description: A recorded effect trace: the ordered observations of one effectful run. Each entry is what one effectful builtin performed (`effect`, an op-specific `detail`) and what the world answered (`result`, a value-expression AST — the same encoding as everywhere else, so replay decodes it directly). A trace is a HASHLESS, SELF-ADDRESSING artifact like a body expression: the whole object is JCS-canonicalized and BLAKE3-hashed to a `trc_…` content-address, which is how an `observed` claim (claim-expression.schema.json) references it. Traces are unsigned — they are content-addressed evidence referenced by a *signed* assert; forging a trace only produces a different address. Secrets never appear: an `http` header carrying `{{secret:NAME}}` is recorded in placeholder form (credentials are effect-boundary configuration, spec/agent-loop.md §Scope), so a trace is publishable and replay needs no secrets. Determinism: replay serves each effect from the next entry in order (strict FIFO, effect names must match, the trace must be consumed exactly), which makes an effectful run re-executable by anyone — principle 5 — without granting any capability.

ops.description: The run's observations, in execution order. May be empty in principle, but an empty trace never occurs in practice: a run that performed no effects yields an ordinary `predicate` claim, not an `observed` one.

effect.description: The capability the op exercised (io.console, time, random, alloc, fs.read, fs.write, net.read, net.write, process.spawn, …) — the same vocabulary as signature.effects.

detail.description: Op-specific description of WHAT was performed (e.g. an http op's method/url/headers/body, with any secret header value still in `{{secret:NAME}}` placeholder form). Advisory for readers; replay matches on `effect` and sequence.

result.description: What the effect returned, as a value-expression AST (value-expression.schema.json). Absent means the op returned unit.
